Social-XP

Goals

Supported Sites

DOM tracking rules

X / Threads / Bluesky

Tracks composer submits for new posts and reply flows when the surrounding UI indicates a reply context.

LinkedIn / Reddit

Tracks published posts plus comment and reply submissions from visible composer forms.

Facebook

Tracks new posts plus comment and reply submissions from visible composer flows and page-specific forms.

Discord

Tracks sent messages and uses the active reply banner to distinguish replies from regular channel posts.

Tracking is DOM-based and best-effort. If a platform changes its UI labels or composer structure, selectors may need a tune-up.

Goal Matrix

Targets by cadence

Period New posts Replies / comments XP target
0 XP
0 XP
0 XP
0 XP

Focus Setup

Set the targets once, then get back to posting.

Social-XP keeps live progress on the page while you write. This view is only for defining daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly targets.

New Post 20 XP

Published post

Reply 8 XP

Comment or reply

Bonus XP Goals + streaks + overgoal

Daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly clears scale with difficulty. Streaks and overgoal bonuses stack on top.

Social-XP Economy

How leveling and bonus XP work

Action XP is fixed, and bonus XP rewards consistency. Changing your goals only changes future goal bonuses, not the XP you already earned.

Core XP

  • New post: 20 XP
  • Reply or comment: 8 XP

How to earn more XP

  • Post consistently across the week instead of relying on one big day.
  • Replies stack fast, so engagement matters as much as publishing.
  • Hitting your targets and going past them adds extra reward XP.

Goal bonuses

Clearing your daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly targets gives bonus XP on top of your normal post and reply XP.

Setting harder goals can increase those future goal bonuses a bit, so ambitious targets feel more rewarding without breaking the level system.

Streak bonus

  • 3+ day streak: +5 XP
  • 7+ day streak: +10 XP
  • 14+ day streak: +15 XP
  • 30+ day streak: +20 XP

Overgoal bonus

  • 125% of daily goal XP: +10 XP
  • 150% of daily goal XP: +15 XP
  • 200% of daily goal XP: +25 XP

How leveling feels

Early levels move quickly so progress feels immediate. Higher levels take more consistency, streaks, and goal clears to reach.

Your old XP stays locked in, so changing your goals later will not rewrite your past levels.
